profile.name: Do Hyun Im
profile.title: AI-Empowered Product Builder
profile.tagline: If you don't like what's out there in the market, build one yourself. No reason not to, right?
profile.bio: I am a builder driven by curiosity and execution. While I didn't study computer science, I realized that traditional coding skills are no longer the boundary to great product building. My true strengths lie in operational planning, ruthless execution, and delivering products in incredibly fast turnaround times. By leveraging deep operational know-how and advanced AI tools to streamline the entire process, I turn market gaps into sustainable, scalable businesses at unprecedented speed.

projects.name: Michin Kimchi
projects.description: Frustrated with the lack of authentic kimchi in Singapore and the high costs of platforms like Shopify for home businesses, I built my own end-to-end e-commerce platform.
details: Started as an MVP using Google Sheets and transitioned to a production-level MMP.
Built a full-stack architecture with Supabase, Resend, Cloudflare, GitHub, and Stripe.
Developed custom delivery mapping logic using the postal code system and integrated a CRM.
Mastered data correlation, structure building, and production-level workflows.
projects.insight: My thought process: I started this because I couldn't find a platform friendly for home-based businesses that didn't eat into margins with monthly fees ($10-15/mo) before even establishing sustainable MRR. The pivot from a Google Sheet MVP to a Supabase MMP was driven by necessity—I needed robust data correlation and custom delivery mapping logic to handle real-world operations efficiently without relying on expensive, inflexible SaaS platforms.

projects.name: Reef AI (Enlio Studio)
projects.description: Enlio Studio is the shell for many apps to come. Reef AI was born from identifying a market demand for testing master water keys for aquatic life.
details: Leveraged Vercel and Gemini Vision to analyze water data and generate interactive dashboards.
Launched rapidly (0-to-1 in a matter of weeks) to test market validation.
Currently experimenting with marketing and user acquisition strategies.
projects.insight: My thought process: While exploring RAG and GEO optimization for Enlio Studio, I side-tracked into aquatic life communities and spotted a real pain point—testing master water keys. The development was rapid and fun, utilizing Vercel and Gemini Vision to automate data analysis. The biggest lesson here is that building the product is only half the battle; without a strong marketing channel, even a great tool struggles to gain MRR.

projects.name: Ennoic Sphere
projects.description: A massive undertaking to integrate Facebook and Google Ads APIs via Magentic, combined with vibe coding for automated website creation.
details: Designed to fully automate the ad creation and deployment pipeline.
Explored the upper limits of AI-assisted development and API integrations.
Currently halted to focus on individual developer capacity and core products.
projects.insight: My thought process: I wanted to push the boundaries of automated agency work by integrating Facebook and Google Ads APIs. I realized that traditional API integration was too brittle, so I pivoted to using Magentic and the Model Context Protocol (MCP). By exposing these platforms as MCP tools, I could let an AI agent dynamically 'vibe code' the campaigns and website creation. However, as an individual developer, the sheer scale of maintaining these agentic workflows took a toll. The key takeaway was understanding capacity limits and the importance of focusing on core products rather than spreading too thin on massive infrastructure projects.

steps.title: Ideation & Gap Analysis
steps.description: Identifying real-world frustrations (like bad Kimchi) or market inefficiencies and conceptualizing a lean solution.
steps.expandedContent: Great products don't start with code; they start with a painful problem. I look for markets where the existing solutions are either too expensive for indie hackers (like Shopify's app ecosystem) or fundamentally broken. I focus intensely on the user experience gap before writing a single line of logic.

steps.title: MVP Construction
steps.description: Starting simple. Validating demand using no-code or low-code tools like Google Sheets before investing heavily in architecture.
steps.expandedContent: I believe in proving the financial model first. For Michin Kimchi, I didn't need a massive database on day one. A Google Sheet and a basic form were enough to prove people would pay for the product. Only after hitting a revenue ceiling do I justify the engineering cost of a custom platform.

steps.title: AI-Assisted Scaling
steps.description: Using tools like Antigravity to rapidly build production-ready architecture (Supabase, Next/Vite, Stripe) that would traditionally take months.
steps.expandedContent: This is where I shine. Once the model is validated, I act as the 'Product Architect' and use AI agents to write the code. By understanding data correlation, component structures, and state management, I can guide tools like Antigravity to build scalable, production-grade architectures in a fraction of the time a traditional team would take.

steps.title: Operation & Sustaining
steps.description: Focusing on the business metrics—marketing, CRM integration, and sustainable revenue—rather than getting bogged down in code.
steps.expandedContent: Code is a liability; revenue is the goal. I structure my apps so they require minimal maintenance, allowing me to focus on growth channels, SEO/GEO optimization, and customer retention. The true test of a builder is whether their product survives the marketing phase.
